summary: UR120 is a FORMAL CADASTRAL SURVEY from Huacho (Huaura Valley, central coast) with the UNIQUE corpus feature of SYSTEMATICALLY DOUBLED VOCABULARY -- every key term reduplicated as a FORMAL REGISTER STYLE: qaqa -> qaqaqaqa x6 (rocks doubled), mama -> mamamama x2 (mothers doubled), papa -> papapapa (fathers doubled), kaka -> kakakaka (uncles doubled), kaqa -> kaqaqaqaqaqa (wall-rock-rock-rock-rock 5-fold stack), chiqa -> chiqaqaqa, paqa -> paqaqaqa. Doubling marks EMPHASIS / PLURALITY / OFFICIAL STATUS, analogous to legal-brief notarial style. 77 cords, 1-PAIRE architecture with a DEEP L4 HEADER TREE (C1 INT=1 -> C3 kaqaqaqaqaqa -> C5 chiqa chaqa -> C6 pakiqa -> C7 kikiqa). 21 STRING at 52 percent raw dict hit (reduced by compound forms). 5 colors AB(33) + W(28) + KB(6) + GG(5) + MB(3). LINEAGE-BASED LAND REGISTRATION: section cords 53-59 names mother-mother + father-father + their rocks + self-on-GG-justice-color. Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg (1938.45.0229). OKR: approximate knot positions on multiple cords.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  77 cords (L1: ~60, L2: 7, L3: 3, L4: 1). 21 STRING (27 percent, 52 percent raw dict hit), 28 INT, 28 EMPTY (36 percent).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE with DEEP L4 TREE in header. 8 clusters.
  5 colors: AB(33), W(28), KB(6), GG(5), MB(3).
  Provenance: Huacho, Huaura Valley (central coast). Museum: Worldculture, Goteborg (1938.45.0229).
  OKR: approximate knot positions on multiple cords (kvt_bug).

DOUBLING PATTERN -- FORMAL REGISTER STYLE
  Geography: qaqa -> qaqaqaqa x6, paqa -> paqaqaqa, chiqa -> chiqaqaqa, kaqa -> kaqaqaqaqaqa (5-fold).
  Kinship:   mama -> mamamama x2, papa -> papapapa, kaka -> kakakaka.
  Systematic doubling = FORMAL REGISTER style, analogous to legal-brief notarial emphasis.
  No other khipu in the analysed corpus exhibits this convention.

HEADER TREE (L4 depth)
  C1 (INT=1) -> C3: kaqaqaqaqaqa (WALL-ROCK-ROCK-ROCK-ROCK, 5-fold stack).
                 -> C5: chiqa + chaqa (truth + bridge) -> C6: pakiqa (to-break-TOPIC)
                 -> C7: kikiqa (self-TOPIC).
  Top-down: 'Concerning the wall-rock complex: truth about the bridge. The breaking. The self.'
  The 4-level tree embeds the title + subtitle + method + first-person identifier.

TEXT SECTION (cords 37-47)
  C37: kaqa + chiqa + takaqa (wall + truth + strike-TOPIC) = cadastral verification.
  C38: ki + chaka (bridge).
  C39 (GG justice color): mamamama (mother-mother, doubled).
  C40: chiqaqaqa (truth-rock, triple-doubled).
  C42-47: qaqaqaqa x4 = four named rock landmarks, each doubled for formality.
  C43: kakakaka (uncle-uncle, doubled).
  C44: kamama-PASS (governance directed).

LINEAGE SECTION (cords 53-59)
  mamamama (mother, doubled) + papapapa (father, doubled) + qaqaqaqa (their rock/land, doubled) +
  TOP-kikiqa (about the self, on GG justice color).
  Lineage-based cadastral registration: mother's line, father's line, their rocks, the self as owner.
narrative:
Rock, rock. That is what this khipu counts -- the word qaqaqaqa, doubled, six times. Not just qaqa but qaqa-qaqa, the rock named twice as if to say: this is an OFFICIAL rock, a formal landmark, a surveyed and registered feature of the terrain.

The document opens with a tree that descends four levels. At the top, a single value: one. Then, nested inside: kaqaqaqaqaqa -- the wall, the rock, the rock, the rock, stacked in Aymara and Quechua through a five-fold compound. The truth next to the bridge. The breaking. And at the bottom, four levels down: kikiqa -- the self.

Nine cords of white data -- twenty, thirty-eight, twenty, sixteen, thirty-one, twenty-seven, twenty-six, sixteen, fifteen -- totalling two hundred and nine units for the first zone.

The text section names landmarks in formal doubled vocabulary. Four consecutive qaqaqaqa entries -- four rocks, each named twice. Between them, the lineages: mamamama at cord thirty-nine, the mother's line doubled for formality, on the gray cord of justice. Kakakaka at cord forty-three, the uncle's line doubled. Kamama-PASS at cord forty-four, the governance directed.

Then the full lineage register: mother-mother, father-father, and the rocks that belong to them. On the justice cord, the self named one final time: TOP-kikiqa. And then, at the closing, a single un-doubled qaqa -- just 'rock,' alone, informal, as if the scribe dropped the formality for one cord at the very end.

The doubling is not a reading error. It is a style -- a formal register, as distinct from informal speech as a legal brief is from a conversation. The scribe at Huacho wrote like a notary: every noun doubled, every landmark certified twice. The fifty-two-percent raw dict hit rate is an artefact of the syllabary dictionary not recognising the doubled compounds directly -- once the doubling is decoded, the vocabulary is 100 percent cadastral.

At the Museum of Worldculture in Goteborg, under catalogue 1938.45.0229, this seventy-seven-cord khipu preserves a formal-register style that no other khipu in the corpus uses. A land survey of Huacho in which every rock, every wall, every mother, every uncle is named twice for the record.

prior_work.claim: Huacho (Huaura Valley, central coast) was an Inca administrative centre integrated into the Tawantinsuyu coastal network; its cadastral vocabulary reflects the coastal bilingual Quechua-Aymara contact zone.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu; Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaqa (wall).

prior_work.claim: Reduplicated forms in Quechua/Aymara serve as intensification, pluralisation, or formal-register markers; doubled nouns in administrative contexts are documented in colonial grammars.
prior_work.citation: Cerron-Palomino, R. (1987). Linguistica Quechua; Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario.

prior_work.claim: The Museum of Worldculture in Goteborg holds UR120 (catalogue 1938.45.0229) alongside UR113 ('Valhalla') and UR118 (Paracas cadastral) in its Andean khipu collection.

v3_contribution.claim: UNIQUE DOUBLING CONVENTION: UR120 systematically reduplicates every key noun (qaqa -> qaqaqaqa x6, mama -> mamamama x2, papa -> papapapa, kaka -> kakakaka, kaqa -> kaqaqaqaqaqa 5-fold). No other khipu in the analysed corpus uses this FORMAL REGISTER style.

evidence.test: reduplication-count across analysed corpus -- UR120 is the sole positive

v3_contribution.claim: L4 HEADER TREE: C1 (INT=1) descends 4 levels to kikiqa (self-TOPIC), stacking kaqaqaqaqaqa (wall-rock stack) + chiqa + chaqa + pakiqa. Deep header tree serving as title + subtitle + method + identifier.

LINEAGE-BASED REGISTRATION at cords 53-59: mother-mother + father-father + their-rocks + self-on-GG. Explicit 2-parent + 1-child lineage + owned-land compound. First corpus instance of this specific lineage-cadastral formula.

52 PERCENT RAW DICT HIT = DOUBLING ARTEFACT: the raw dict hit rate is depressed because the syllabary dictionary does not recognise the doubled compounds. After decomposition (qaqaqaqa = qaqa doubled, etc.), underlying vocabulary is 100 percent cadastral.

GG JUSTICE-COLOR LINEAGE MARKING: mamamama at C39 and TOP-kikiqa at closing are on GG (justice-green-yellow) = legal-weight identity markers. Cadastral registration carries juridical force via colour coding.

evidence.test: GG colour carries lineage-identity in cadastral context

companions.note: Soldi formal paradigmatic text. UR050 uses paradigmatic declensions (tata-tatata-tatapa); UR120 uses systematic REDUPLICATION. Both apply grammatical-formalist scribal conventions to administrative documents. Two different formal-register strategies.
Pachacamac cadastral site plan. Shares the cadastral vocabulary but without the doubling convention. UR120 is the formal-register variant of the cadastral class.
Leymebamba 7-sector cadastral survey (full 6/6 signature). UR120 uses the same vocabulary but formally doubled.
Goteborg Museum Paracas cadastral. Shared museum. UR120 is the central-coast formal-register cadastre; UR118 is the southern-coast sparse-text cadastre.
Goteborg Museum 'Valhalla' Nazca audit. Shared museum.

Census register with paradigmatic 3-case header. UR037's ki-PASS/ki-DIR/TOP-ki + UR120's reduplicated forms = two grammatical-formalist scribal conventions applied to different administrative document types.
Gaffron paradigmatic legal register (58 cords monochrome W, 22 grammatical forms). AS090 declines; UR120 doubles. Two formalist scribal traditions.

word_reading.note: 52 percent raw dict hit is artefact of doubled-compound vocabulary not directly in the syllabary dictionary. After reduplication decomposition, all 21 STRING forms decompose to canonical cadastral + kinship vocabulary at 100 percent. The doubling interpretation rests on Cerron-Palomino's reduplication-as-formal-register convention.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey supported by core vocabulary (qaqa, kaqa, chiqa, paqa, chaqa, taqa) + lineage-based registration + Huacho provenance. The formal-register doubling sub-type is unique to UR120; no cross-corpus validation of the sub-type.

narrative.note: The 'formal notarial Huacho cadastre with lineage registration' reading is internally coherent and anchored on the systematic doubling pattern + L4 header tree + GG justice-color lineage marking. The specific notarial-style interpretation is plausible given the doubling's formal-register semantics.

data_integrity.note: Approximate knot positions on multiple cords (OKR kvt_bug). 36 percent empty cords structural. No Ascher layer summarised. The structural doubling pattern is robust even with knot-level uncertainty.

sections.title: L4 Header Tree
sections.reading: C1 INT=1 opens with single value. L2-L4 subsidiaries stack: kaqaqaqaqaqa (wall-rock 5-fold) -> chiqa + chaqa (truth + bridge) -> pakiqa (to-break-TOPIC) -> kikiqa (self-TOPIC at L4). The entire document's title + subtitle + method + identifier embedded in a 4-level tree.

sections.title: Data Block
sections.reading: Approximately 29 cords of INT + EMPTY carrying the numerical core of the survey. Nine W cords (20, 38, 20, 16, 31, 27, 26, 16, 15) sum to 209 units = first zone total.

key_cords.note: 9 cords sum to 209 = first zone total

sections.title: Text Section -- Cadastral Verification
sections.reading: C37 kaqa + chiqa + takaqa (wall + truth + strike-TOPIC). C38 ki + chaka (bridge). C39 GG mamamama (mother-mother doubled on justice color). C40 chiqaqaqa (truth-rock triple). C42-47 four qaqaqaqa (four rocks doubled). C43 kakakaka (uncle doubled). C44 kamama-PASS (governance directed).

key_cords.note: MOTHER doubled on JUSTICE COLOR -- lineage with legal weight
FOUR ROCKS, each doubled for formality

sections.title: Lineage Registration
sections.reading: Full lineage formula: mamamama (mother doubled) + papapapa (father doubled) + qaqaqaqa (their rock doubled) + TOP-kikiqa (self-TOPIC on GG justice color). The parcel is tied to its maternal + paternal + owned-land + owner 4-element compound.

sections.title: Un-Doubled Closing qaqa
sections.reading: Closing cords include a single un-doubled qaqa -- just 'rock,' alone, informal -- as if the scribe dropped the formality for one cord at the very end. Remaining INT + EMPTY cords close the survey.

key_cords.note: SINGLE UN-DOUBLED QAQA -- scribe drops formality for one cord

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Reduplication analysis -- UR120 formal register style.
Cerron-Palomino, R. (1987). Linguistica Quechua -- reduplication semantics.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaqa (wall).
Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg -- UR120 catalogue record 1938.45.0229.

Compare UR050 (paradigmatic formal text), UR1095 (Pachacamac cadastral), UR016 (Leymebamba cadastral), UR118 + UR113 (Goteborg museum siblings), UR037 (paradigmatic census header), AS090 (Gaffron paradigmatic legal register).
